items.title: Employment services to get largest overhaul in 30 years

children.content: Jobseeker services will shift away from a one-size-fits-all approach to a new tiered model, to be unveiled by the government later today.

articles.title: Iran war derailed talks to claw back billions in tax credits paid to miners

children.content: Billions in tax credits are paid to coal miners every year. Ministers discussed clawing back some of that money in the May federal budget, but the Iran war has pushed out those talks.

image.altText: A female mine worker walks past a mine dump truck at a coal mine in central Queensland.

articles.title: Calls to raise CGT concession threshold to $10m for small business

children.content: Small Business Minister Anne Aly says consultation with start-ups and small businesses is ongoing, but doesn't give much away as the peak small business body urges the government to expand capital gains tax concessions. Follow live.

image.altText: Aly leans over a desk as she speaks on the lower house floor.

gallery.altText: A blurry image of people queueing to enter a Centrelink office to apply for unemployment benefits.

thumbnail.altText: A blurry image of people queueing to enter a Centrelink office to apply for unemployment benefits.

items.title: Woman forced out of home as mould grows after insurer repairs roof

children.content: Lily lodged an insurance claim with her provider GIO Suncorp after a truck ran through her street's power lines. Nearly a decade later, she has been forced out of her home.

image.altText: Lily looks at the camera in her garden wearing a red shirt

items.title: Westpac fined $26m for 'grossly negligent' conduct, ASX heading for cautious start

children.content: The Federal Court imposes a $26 million fine on Westpac for failing to respond quickly to customers facing financial hardship, while the Australian share market is likely to start its day relatively flat after Iran accused the United States of violating its fragile ceasefire and warned of retaliation. Follow live.

image.altText: People walking past a Westpac sign.

items.title: Fidel Castro's daughter fled his regime, and has a warning for the US

children.content: Fidel Castro's daughter says the US has failed to find a way to deal with Cuba for decades. But she tells the ABC that the Trump administration has a difference that could spell the end of the regime she fled.

gallery.altText: A photo of a young woman in a white lace dress, posing with a man in a green military uniform and thick black beard.
A 70-year-old woman in a denim jacket looks solemn, while holding decorative steel railing outside a home, surrounded by trees.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of a young woman in a white lace dress, posing with a man in a green military uniform and thick black beard.
A 70-year-old woman in a denim jacket looks solemn, while holding decorative steel railing outside a home, surrounded by trees.

items.title: Women and children linked to ISIS spend first night back in Australia

children.content: A group of so-called ISIS brides who left the notorious al-Roj camp in Syria last week have spent their first night home in Australia.

image.altText: A woman in back seat of a car wearing a face mask.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

image.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.

items.title: Why drones fell from the sky during Sydney's Vivid light show

children.content: The company behind the drone shows at Sydney's Vivid festival said spectators were not at risk when almost 90 unmanned aircraft fell from the sky during a display on Monday night.

image.altText: Screengrab from mobile phone video shows purple drones in ragged formation over body of water with city in background at night

gallery.altText: Screengrab from mobile phone video shows purple drones in ragged formation over body of water with city in background at night

thumbnail.altText: Screengrab from mobile phone video shows purple drones in ragged formation over body of water with city in background at night

avatar.alt: Profile picture ABC reporter Alex Lewis

items.title: Iran calls latest US strikes 'bad faith' sign as internet slowly restored

children.content: Iran has denounced US strikes in the country's south as a sign of "bad faith and unreliability" as negotiations toward a possible deal to end the war continue.

image.altText: Smoke billows across a city.

items.title: Australian students record worst tech literacy results on record

children.content: Australian students have recorded their worst-ever results in national tests that measure digital literacy, with just 37 per cent of year 10 students and 50 per cent of year 6 students assessed as proficient.

image.altText: A pair of unidentifiable hands type on a laptop, with other people on laptops in the background.

items.title: Hotspots reveal temperature danger zone for koala survival

children.content: The risks of death or hospitalisation of koalas increases as soon as seven-day average maximum temperature is above 27 degrees Celsius, a new study shows.

gallery.altText: Landscape shot of a gap in two trees with a koala head in between with its eyes closed under harsh sunlight.

thumbnail.altText: Landscape shot of a gap in two trees with a koala head in between with its eyes closed under harsh sunlight.

avatar.alt: A man in a blue checked shirt and blue jacket with a beard and long hair.

items.title: Rain brings potential 'opportunities' in renewed search for Gus Lamont

children.content: Eight months since Gus Lamont was reported missing, police have again returned to search Oak Park Station as part of an ongoing investigations into the four-year-old's disappearance.

image.altText: Gus Lamont

items.title: Two prospective Whyalla steelworks buyers remain, as sale reaches 'final stages'

children.content: The federal and state governments have reduced the number of bidders for the financially stricken Whyalla steelworks from five to two.

image.altText: Workers at the Whyalla steelworks blast furnace.

items.title: Two men released without charge after Dezi Freeman-related arrest

children.content: A 48-year-old and a 45-year-old were taken into custody by detectives from Taskforce Summit, who continued to investigate Freeman's movements after the fatal shooting of officers Neal Thompson and Vadim de Waart-Hottart at a Porepunkah property in Victoria's north-east in August last year.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: 'Not far away': Rescuers confident of reaching group trapped in Laos cave

children.content: Seven people remain trapped after heavy rain triggered a landslide but rescuers say they are close to reaching the group.

image.altText: Four men stand at a narrow cave entrance wearing helmets and carrying ropes.

items.title: Next steps to take after receiving life-altering ancestry DNA test results

children.content: Direct-to-consumer genetic testing kits are often given as gifts or purchased "as a bit of fun", which means people often aren't prepared to discover unexpected parents, siblings and other relatives.

gallery.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

thumbnail.altText: man looking at phone with shocked expression

avatar.alt: Kellie Scott black and white headshot

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: More or Less quiz: We need you to rank five State of Origin greats

children.content: If you think you know your stuff when it comes to State of Origin, here's your chance to prove it.

items.title: Scott's sacking gives Essendon another chance to step out of Hird's shadow

children.content: Essendon finds itself at a clear demarcation point after sacking Brad Scott as the shadow of James Hird once again looms large over the troubled club.

articles.title: Scott reveals he wanted to trade Merrett, confirms bumper Reid offer

children.content: The ousted Essendon coach reveals he was against the decision to keep Zach Merrett at the club despite a late trade offer from Hawthorn.

image.altText: Brad Scott and Zach Merrett stand together on Anzac Day

gallery.altText: James Hird scratches his chin with his right hand

thumbnail.altText: James Hird scratches his chin with his right hand

items.title: 'Did you get any of it right?': Senator calls out handling of $15m court contract

children.content: Litigants in at least 146 court matters were potentially involved in a data breach that is now the subject of a formal complaint with the privacy commissioner in Canada, Senate estimates has been told.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and glasses and maroon tie speaking into microphone.

items.title: Report warns AI boom could keep Australia using coal and gas for longer

children.content: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.

image.altText: a man walks between rows of servers

items.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open

children.content: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

image.altText: Adam Walton, wearing a white shirt, holds his hands in the air and smiles.

items.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct

children.content: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and his "overbearing" behaviour, something he later described as a "false narrative".

image.altText: A man smiling in a suit.

items.title: Department accused of 'poor decision making' over $6.2m helmet rollout

children.content: Queensland's fire minister has intervened following tensions between the firefighter union and department.

gallery.altText: A man in a fire fighting uniform wearing a fluro yellow helmet in front of a fire truck.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a fire fighting uniform wearing a fluro yellow helmet in front of a fire truck.

avatar.alt: A portrait shot of Eden standing next to a tree.

items.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice

children.content: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.

image.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal

items.title: At least 11 dead as Europe bakes in unprecedented heatwave

children.content: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.

image.altText: A man drinking from a water bottle out the front of Big Ben.

items.title: 'Can't put it into words': Origin star's mum reveals heartbreaking call

children.content: Cameron Munster's mum reveals the heartbreaking call she had to make before her superstar son led Queensland to victory days after the shock death of his dad.

gallery.altText: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after this year's Origin decider.

thumbnail.altText: A tearful Queensland coach Billy Slater hugs skipper Cameron Munster after this year's Origin decider.

items.title: 'Kidding ourselves': Daley whacks talk of Cleary's losing Origin record

children.content: NSW coach Laurie Daley says the responsibility does not solely rest on the shoulders of his star No.7 ahead of this year's State of Origin campaign.

image.altText: Nathan Cleary stands with his hands on his hips

items.title: The NSW Blues made a 'tough' selection call, so will the Wallabies

children.content: Just as NSW Blues coach Laurie Daley had a difficult selection decision to make, Joe Schmidt may be thinking long and hard about one position in the Wallabies' back line for July's Nations Championship Tests.

image.altText: Max Jorgensen runs onto the field for the Waratahs.

items.title: NT paramedics consider body-worn cameras amid ongoing violent assaults

children.content: The Northern Territory's ambulance service is considering introducing police-style body-worn cameras after the arrest of alleged murderer Jefferson Lewis led to rioting in Alice Springs last month.

image.altText: Two paramedics in uniform pose with an ambulance

items.title: Reception hours slashed at police stations in Melbourne's outer-north

children.content: Victoria Police says its decision to cut the hours police stations are open to the public in Melbourne's outer north will result in more police on the frontline.

image.altText: A police badge on an arm.

items.title: Australian underdog stuns former world number one at French Open

children.content: Queenslander Adam Walton makes it through the heat and five-set grind to down former world number one Daniil Medvedev at the French Open.

gallery.altText: Adam Walton, wearing a white shirt, holds his hands in the air and smiles.

thumbnail.altText: Adam Walton, wearing a white shirt, holds his hands in the air and smiles.

items.title: SAPOL commissioner rejects outback police shortage plan

children.content: Commissioner Grant Stevens rejects recommendations to attract more officers to the understaffed Coober Pedy station over funding concerns.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Coober Pedy

items.title: After two decades of addiction, drug court offered Brandie a way out

children.content: The peer support worker at Dubbo's Bunmabunmarra service knows firsthand how the drug court can offer a second chance.

image.altText: Woman standing in front of sign that says Bunmabunmarra

items.title: ACT public school system to be unified in 'generational shift'

children.content: The ACT's public education system will be significantly reformed after a review of resourcing found schools operated much like "independent small businesses".

image.altText: A group of primary school students sit in a classroom facing away.

items.title: Hungry eleven-armed seastars making a meal of Tasmanian scallops

children.content: The fast spread of pest eleven-armed seastars has fishers, along with researchers, concerned.

image.altText: A seastar at Hallett Cove.
